title: Coffea capitata
intro: Coffea capitata is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to Madagascar, and is a member of the coffee family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to alleviate anxiety, insomnia, and other nervous system disorders, due to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Coffea capitata include alkaloids, such as caffeine, as well as xanthines and other phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbalism, Coffea capitata is often prepared as an infusion or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs in formulas to support nervous system health and promote relaxation.
taxonomy: Coffea capitata is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Coffea, which includes around 125 species of coffee plants. The genus Coffea is further classified under the order Gentianales, and the class Magnoliopsida. Coffea capitata is native to Madagascar, and its classification is still being researched and refined by botanists. It is considered to be an endangered species due to habitat loss and other environmental factors.
